item.title: The Black Stork
item.description: The Black Stork, also known as Are You Fit To Marry?, is a 1916 American eugenic propaganda feature film promoting the star's real-life practice of eugenic infanticide and especially the controversy surrounding the death of Allan J. Bollinger.[1][2][3] The film's title references the belief that black storks kill their unhealthy hatchlings.[3]

item.title: Brizard
item.description: Brizard is a surname. Notable people with the surname include:

item.title: Metaweb
item.description: Metaweb Technologies, Inc. was a San Francisco–based company that developed Freebase, described as an "open, shared database of the world's knowledge". The company was co-founded by Danny Hillis, Veda Hlubinka-Cook and John Giannandrea in 2005.[3]

item.title: Alan Harper (American football)
item.description: Alan Stephen Harper (born September 6, 1979) is an American former professional football player who was a defensive tackle for one season with the New York Jets of the National Football League (NFL). He played college football for the Fresno State Bulldogs and was selected by the Jets in the fourth round of the 2002 NFL draft.

item.title: Musa Shuaib
item.description: Musa Muhammad Ali Shuaib (Arabic: موسى شعيب; born 1943) was a Lebanese politician who was a member of the Arab Socialist Ba'ath Party in Lebanon. He was assassinated on 28 July 1980 in a car bomb attack at Beirut International Airport.
